title: Day 1: Set Up Claude & Build Your AIY Framework
problemStatement:
Welcome to Day 1! Today you'll set up your Claude account, explore the interface, and start building your personal AIY (AI for You) framework.

**Tasks:**
1. Sign up for Claude at claude.ai
2. Explore Projects, Memory, and Settings
3. Identify 5 areas where Claude could help you daily
4. Document your AIY framework — a personal map of how you'll use Claude

**Submission:** Share notes, a Claude conversation link, or a short doc that captures your framework.

**Note:** Real content from our team launches before June 1, 2026.
learningObjectives: Understand Claude's core features
Identify personal use cases for AI
Set up your workspace
linkedinTemplate:
Day 1 of #60DayClaudeChallenge with @AnilBajpai's @ABtalks 🚀

Today I set up my Claude account and started mapping my AIY (AI for You) framework — identifying 5 daily areas where AI can make me more productive.

What would YOUR top 5 use cases be? Drop them in the comments 👇

#60DayClaudeChallenge #ABtalks
solutionApproach: Share your AIY framework. Could be a Claude conversation, a doc, or a screenshot of your notes.

title: Day 2: Projects, Memory & Workspace Hygiene
problemStatement:
Welcome to **Day 2** of the 60-Day Claude AI Mastery Challenge.

**Theme:** Phase 1 — Foundation & Prompt Engineering (Orientation)

**Today's focus:** Projects, Memory & Workspace Hygiene

**Tasks:**
1. Use Claude for structured thinking; save useful threads.
2. Document what worked in a simple prompt log.
3. Prepare your share link or notes for submission.

**Submission:** Share a Claude conversation link, artifact, or short doc that shows your work.

**Note:** Real content from our team launches before June 1, 2026.
learningObjectives: Apply Day 2 concepts to your real role or studies
Build repeatable prompts you can reuse after the challenge
Practice safe, reviewable AI workflows
linkedinTemplate:
Day 2/60 of #60DayClaudeChallenge with @ABtalks 🚀

Today (Projects, Memory & Workspace Hygiene): Phase 1 — building real skills, not just hype.

What did you automate or clarify with Claude today? 👇

#60DayClaudeChallenge #ABtalks
solutionApproach: Submit a valid Claude share or artifact URL, plus your LinkedIn post URL when prompted.

dayContent.title: Prompt Architecture
dayContent.tagline: The anatomy of a prompt that works every time

learning.summary: The five building blocks of every strong prompt: Role, Context, Instruction, Output Format, and Structure. You'll learn how reordering or sharpening any one of these changes the result.

bullets.label: Role
bullets.text: tell Claude who to be ("Act as a senior financial analyst…").

bullets.label: Context
bullets.text: give the background it needs (audience, goal, constraints).

bullets.label: Instruction
bullets.text: state the single clear task you want done.

bullets.label: Output Format
bullets.text: specify exactly how the answer should look (table, bullets, word count).

bullets.label: Structure
bullets.text: order these logically so the model never has to guess.

tool.name: MetaPrompt
tool.description: A free Chrome extension that rewrites your rough prompt into an expert, structured one with a single click, right inside Claude.
tool.setupTitle: How to set up MetaPrompt in Claude
setupSteps: Open the Chrome Web Store link below and click "Add to Chrome."
Pin the MetaPrompt icon to your toolbar (click the puzzle-piece icon, then the pin).
Go to claude.ai and type your rough prompt in the message box as usual.
Click the MetaPrompt button that appears near the input — it rewrites your prompt into a structured version.
Review the optimized prompt, then send it to Claude. Free plan gives 20 enhancements per month.

task.title: Your Task
steps: Take any everyday request (e.g. "write a leave application").
Write it first as a lazy one-liner. Save Claude's response.
Now rewrite it using all five building blocks. Save the new response.
Run your structured prompt through MetaPrompt and note what it improved.

dayContent.promptTemplate:
Role: You are a [profession] with 10 years of experience.
Context: I need this for [audience] because [goal].
Constraints: [tone, length, must-include points].
Instruction: Write a [deliverable] that achieves [outcome].
Output Format: [bullets / table / 150-word paragraph].
Structure: Start with [X], then [Y], end with [Z].

engagement.description: Post your "lazy prompt vs structured prompt" side by side and the two different outputs. Caption it: what surprised you most about the difference?

title: Day 3: Chain-of-Thought & Step-by-Step Reasoning
problemStatement:
Welcome to **Day 3** of the 60-Day Claude AI Mastery Challenge.

**Theme:** Phase 1 — Foundation & Prompt Engineering

**Today's focus:** Chain-of-Thought Prompting

**Tasks:**
1. Make Claude reason step-by-step on a complex problem.
2. Install a token counter and compare token usage.
3. Prepare your share link or notes for submission.

**Submission:** Share a Claude conversation link, artifact, or short doc that shows your work.

**Note:** Real content from our team launches before June 1, 2026.
learningObjectives: Apply Day 3 concepts to your real role or studies
Build repeatable prompts you can reuse after the challenge
Practice safe, reviewable AI workflows
linkedinTemplate:
Day 3/60 of #60DayClaudeChallenge with @ABtalks 🚀

Today (Chain-of-Thought Prompting): I made AI reason step-by-step — and watched how many tokens "thinking" actually costs.

What complex problem did you crack with Claude today? 👇

#60DayClaudeChallenge #ABtalks
solutionApproach: Submit a valid Claude share or artifact URL, plus your LinkedIn post URL when prompted.

dayContent.title: Chain-of-Thought Prompting
dayContent.tagline: Make Claude reason step-by-step for hard problems

learning.summary: Multi-step reasoning prompts. Instead of asking for an answer, you ask Claude to think through a problem in stages — dramatically improving accuracy on complex tasks.

bullets.label: Why it works
bullets.text: “think step by step” unlocks better answers on math, logic, and planning tasks.

bullets.label: Decompose
bullets.text: how to break a big problem into ordered sub-questions.

bullets.label: Separate reasoning
bullets.text: how to ask Claude to show its reasoning, then give a final answer separately.

bullets.label: Token awareness
bullets.text: how longer reasoning uses more tokens — and how to watch that with a token counter.

tool.name: Claude Usage Tracker
tool.description: A free Chrome extension that shows a live token count and your session/weekly usage on claude.ai, so you can see what “reasoning” costs against the 200K limit.
tool.setupTitle: How to set up the token counter in Claude
setupSteps: Open the Chrome Web Store link below and click "Add to Chrome."
Go to claude.ai and open any conversation.
The extension injects a live token count and usage bars directly into the Claude interface — no setup or API key needed.
Watch the counter as you send messages; note the number before and after a chain-of-thought prompt.

task.title: Your Task
steps: Install the Claude Usage Tracker extension (link in the box above).
Pick a genuinely complex problem (a budget plan, a tricky decision, a multi-step calculation).
Solve it with a normal prompt. Note the token count.
Solve it again with a chain-of-thought prompt. Compare both the quality and the token count.

dayContent.promptTemplate:
I need to solve: [your complex problem].

Think through this step by step:
  1. First, identify what we know and what we need.
  2. Then break the problem into smaller parts.
  3. Solve each part, showing your reasoning.
  4. Finally, give me the answer in a clear summary.

Show your reasoning first, then a clearly labelled FINAL ANSWER.

engagement.description: Share a screenshot of your token counter on a normal answer vs a chain-of-thought answer. Tell people how many tokens “thinking” cost — and whether it was worth it.

title: Day 4: Role-Based Prompting & Context Capsules
problemStatement:
Welcome to **Day 4** of the 60-Day Claude AI Mastery Challenge.

**Theme:** Phase 1 — Foundation & Prompt Engineering

**Today's focus:** Role-Based Prompting

**Tasks:**
1. Turn Claude into three different experts for the same problem.
2. Save a chat's context as a capsule and reuse it in a fresh chat.
3. Prepare your share link or notes for submission.

**Submission:** Share a Claude conversation link, artifact, or short doc that shows your work.

**Note:** Real content from our team launches before June 1, 2026.
learningObjectives: Apply Day 4 concepts to your real role or studies
Build repeatable prompts you can reuse after the challenge
Practice safe, reviewable AI workflows
linkedinTemplate:
Day 4/60 of #60DayClaudeChallenge with @ABtalks 🚀

Today (Role-Based Prompting): same question, three expert personas — wildly different answers.

Which "expert" would you trust most? 👇

#60DayClaudeChallenge #ABtalks
solutionApproach: Submit a valid Claude share or artifact URL, plus your LinkedIn post URL when prompted.

dayContent.title: Role-Based Prompting
dayContent.tagline: Turn Claude into any expert you need

learning.summary: “Act-as” personas. By assigning Claude a specific professional identity (consultant, developer, analyst, lawyer) you get sharper, domain-aware answers.

bullets.label: Personas shift output
bullets.text: how a well-defined role changes vocabulary, depth, and assumptions in Claude's answers.

bullets.label: Layering
bullets.text: how to layer a role with experience level, industry, and audience.

bullets.label: Carry context
bullets.text: why starting a fresh chat (to save tokens) usually means losing context — and how a capsule extension fixes that by carrying the important context across.

tool.name: Capsule Hub by Tilantra
tool.description: A free Chrome extension that captures a chat's context as a reusable “capsule” you can drag into a fresh Claude chat, so you keep the persona and context without hitting the token limit.
tool.setupTitle: How to set up Capsule Hub in Claude
setupSteps: Open the Chrome Web Store link below and click "Add to Chrome." Pin the icon to your toolbar.
In a Claude chat you want to save, click the Capsule Hub capture button to save the core context as a Capsule.
Open a brand-new Claude chat (this resets your token usage).
Drag your saved Capsule from the Capsule Hub library into the Claude message box — Claude instantly understands the background, goals, and persona.
Continue working in the fresh chat with full context but a clean token budget.

task.title: Your Task
steps: Choose a real problem from your life or studies (e.g. "review my resume", "plan my week").
Solve it three times with three different expert personas. Compare the differences.
Install Capsule Hub, save your best chat as a capsule, and start a fresh chat by dragging that capsule in — confirm the persona and context carried over.

dayContent.promptTemplate:
Act as a [senior role] with deep experience in [industry].
Your communication style is [direct / supportive / analytical].
Your audience is [who will read this].

Task: [what you need].
Give me your expert recommendation, then list the 3 risks
a less experienced person would miss.

engagement.description: Post the same question answered by three different “experts.” Ask your audience which persona gave the most useful answer — and why.

deliverable.description: A 3-persona comparison + a saved context capsule

title: Day 5: Context Management & Token Limits
problemStatement:
Welcome to **Day 5** of the 60-Day Claude AI Mastery Challenge.

**Theme:** Phase 1 — Foundation & Prompt Engineering

**Today's focus:** Context Management

**Tasks:**
1. Write a compact "memory block" that carries a long chat forward.
2. Use the caveman technique to save tokens.
3. Prepare your share link or notes for submission.

**Submission:** Share a Claude conversation link, artifact, or short doc that shows your work.

**Note:** Real content from our team launches before June 1, 2026.
learningObjectives: Apply Day 5 concepts to your real role or studies
Build repeatable prompts you can reuse after the challenge
Practice safe, reviewable AI workflows
linkedinTemplate:
Day 5/60 of #60DayClaudeChallenge with @ABtalks 🚀

Today (Context Management): learned to keep Claude on-track across long chats with a tiny "memory block" — and the caveman trick to slash tokens.

How do you manage long AI conversations? 👇

#60DayClaudeChallenge #ABtalks
solutionApproach: Submit a valid Claude share or artifact URL, plus your LinkedIn post URL when prompted.

dayContent.title: Context Management
dayContent.tagline: Run long conversations without losing the thread

learning.summary: Long-conversation threading, memory tricks, and token limits. Learn how to keep Claude on track across a long session and what to do as you approach the context limit.

bullets.label: Context window
bullets.text: what the 200K-token context window actually means in practice.

bullets.label: Memory block
bullets.text: how to summarize earlier parts of a chat into a compact “memory block” you re-paste.

bullets.label: Caveman technique
bullets.text: writing context in terse keywords (“user = student. goal = resume. tone = formal.”) to save tokens.

bullets.label: Fresh vs thread
bullets.text: when to start fresh vs when to keep threading.

tool.name: Caveman (technique + Claude Code skill)
tool.description: Compress your context into ultra-short, keyword-style notes, and have Claude reply the same way to cut tokens dramatically. There's even a one-click caveman skill for Claude Code users.
tool.setupTitle: How to use the caveman technique
setupSteps: For everyone (no install): in any claude.ai chat, write your context in terse caveman style and add “reply in caveman style — keep all technical detail, cut filler words.” Claude's shorter answers use far fewer tokens.
Why it works: filler like “Sure, I'd be happy to help…” wastes tokens. Caveman keeps the facts, code, and exact terms — just removes the fluff (often ~75% shorter output).
For Claude Code users only: install it as a one-line skill — run  claude install-skill JuliusBrussee/caveman  then trigger it with  /caveman  or “caveman mode.”
Turn it off any time with “normal mode.”
Note: this is a free community skill, not an official Anthropic tool — use it for the technique, which is what matters.
tool.linkLabel: View the caveman skill on GitHub

task.title: Your Task
steps: Take a long chat you've had with Claude (or build one).
Write a 5-line "memory block" that captures everything important in caveman style.
Start a new chat, paste the memory block, and continue seamlessly.
Note how many tokens the caveman version saved versus pasting the whole history.

dayContent.promptTemplate:
CONTEXT MEMORY:
  who = [you / your role]
  goal = [the main objective]
  done so far = [key decisions, in 3-5 keywords each]
  constraints = [tone, format, deadlines]
  next = [what we were about to do]

Continue from here. Confirm you understand the context first.

engagement.description: Share your “memory block” and explain how it let you continue a long conversation in a fresh chat without losing context. Bonus: show the token savings.

deliverable.description: A reusable context memory block + token-savings note

title: Day 6: Professional Email Writing
problemStatement:
Welcome to **Day 6** of the 60-Day Claude AI Mastery Challenge.

**Theme:** Phase 2 — Business Communication & Email Writing

**Today's focus:** Professional Email Writing

**Tasks:**
1. Write one real email yourself, then improve it with Claude.
2. Generate a 5-template email library.
3. Prepare your share link or notes for submission.

**Submission:** Share a Claude conversation link, artifact, or short doc that shows your work.

**Note:** Real content from our team launches before June 1, 2026.
learningObjectives: Apply Day 6 concepts to your real role or studies
Build repeatable prompts you can reuse after the challenge
Practice safe, reviewable AI workflows
linkedinTemplate:
Day 6/60 of #60DayClaudeChallenge with @ABtalks 🚀

Today (Professional Email Writing): wrote an email myself, then rebuilt it with Claude. The before/after is wild.

What's the one email you send most often? 👇

#60DayClaudeChallenge #ABtalks
solutionApproach: Submit a valid Claude share or artifact URL, plus your LinkedIn post URL when prompted.

dayContent.title: Professional Email Writing
dayContent.tagline: Five email templates every professional needs

learning.summary: How to generate five core email types with Claude: formal request, follow-up, apology, status update, and meeting request. You'll learn to control tone, seniority, and length.

bullets.label: Email structure
bullets.text: the structure of a high-converting professional email: subject, hook, ask, close.

bullets.label: Tone control
bullets.text: how to set tone and recipient seniority explicitly in your prompt.

bullets.label: Reusable template
bullets.text: how to generate a reusable template you can adapt for any situation.

tool.name: Before vs After
tool.description: Write one email yourself first, then improve it with AI. Compare clarity, professionalism, tone, and time saved.
tool.setupTitle: How to run the Before vs After
setupSteps: Pick one real email you need to send this week.
Write the “before” version yourself, no AI. Save it.
Generate an improved “after” version with the prompt template.
Put them side by side and note what improved: clarity, tone, professionalism, time saved.

task.title: Your Task
steps: Pick one real email you need to send this week.
Write it yourself first, without AI. Save this "before" version.
Use the prompt template to generate an improved version with Claude.
Generate all five email templates so you have a personal library.

dayContent.promptTemplate:
Write a professional [email type: follow-up / apology / update]
from me to [recipient + seniority].
Context: [what happened / what I need].
Tone: [warm but professional]. Length: under [120] words.
Include a clear subject line and a single specific call to action.

engagement.description: Post your email “before AI vs after AI” side by side. Highlight what improved: clarity, tone, professionalism, and how much time you saved.

deliverable.description: Five-email template pack (formal, follow-up, apology, update, meeting request)

title: Day 7: Cold Outreach Mastery
problemStatement:
Welcome to **Day 7** of the 60-Day Claude AI Mastery Challenge.

**Theme:** Phase 2 — Business Communication & Email Writing

**Today's focus:** Cold Outreach Mastery

**Tasks:**
1. Write your normal cold message, then build a 3-touch sequence with Claude.
2. Personalize the opener so it's about them, not you.
3. Prepare your share link or notes for submission.

**Submission:** Share a Claude conversation link, artifact, or short doc that shows your work.

**Note:** Real content from our team launches before June 1, 2026.
learningObjectives: Apply Day 7 concepts to your real role or studies
Build repeatable prompts you can reuse after the challenge
Practice safe, reviewable AI workflows
linkedinTemplate:
Day 7/60 of #60DayClaudeChallenge with @ABtalks 🚀

Today (Cold Outreach Mastery): turned a bland cold message into a 3-touch sequence that actually earns replies.

Would you reply to this? 👇

#60DayClaudeChallenge #ABtalks
solutionApproach: Submit a valid Claude share or artifact URL, plus your LinkedIn post URL when prompted.

dayContent.title: Cold Outreach Mastery

learning.summary: How to write a cold outreach sequence: an intro message, a follow-up, and a final nudge. You'll learn personalization, brevity, and clear value framing.

bullets.label: Them, not you
bullets.text: why the first line must be about them, not you.

bullets.label: 3-touch structure
bullets.text: intro → follow-up → final nudge, spaced over days.

bullets.label: Brevity
bullets.text: how to keep each message under 80 words and end with one clear ask.

tool.name: Before vs After
tool.description: Share the outreach message you'd normally send vs the AI-improved sequence.
tool.setupTitle: How to run the Before vs After
setupSteps: Choose a real person or company you'd genuinely like to reach.
Write your normal cold message first. Save the “before.”
Generate a full 3-touch sequence with the prompt template.
Compare your single message vs the structured sequence side by side.

task.title: Your Task
steps: Choose a real person or company you'd genuinely like to reach (mentor, recruiter, client).
Write your normal cold message first. Save the "before."
Use the template to generate a full 3-touch sequence with Claude.

dayContent.promptTemplate:
Write a 3-message cold outreach sequence.
From: [you, your one-line value].  To: [recipient + why them].
Goal: [intro call / collaboration / referral].
Message 1: intro (under 80 words, lead with something about them).
Message 2: follow-up after 3 days (new angle, not a nag).
Message 3: final nudge after 5 more days (short, polite, easy yes).

engagement.description: Share your cold outreach “before vs after.” Ask your network: would you reply to this? It sparks great comments.

title: Day 8: Client Proposals
problemStatement:
Welcome to **Day 8** of the 60-Day Claude AI Mastery Challenge.

**Theme:** Phase 2 — Business Communication & Email Writing

**Today's focus:** Client Proposals

**Tasks:**
1. Generate a full client proposal (scope, timeline, pricing).
2. Refine pricing and timeline until realistic.
3. Prepare your share link or notes for submission.

**Submission:** Share a Claude conversation link, artifact, or short doc that shows your work.

**Note:** Real content from our team launches before June 1, 2026.
learningObjectives: Apply Day 8 concepts to your real role or studies
Build repeatable prompts you can reuse after the challenge
Practice safe, reviewable AI workflows
linkedinTemplate:
Day 8/60 of #60DayClaudeChallenge with @ABtalks 🚀

Today (Client Proposals): built a full client-ready proposal — scope, timeline, ROI, pricing — in a fraction of the usual time.

What would you offer as a service? 👇

#60DayClaudeChallenge #ABtalks
solutionApproach: Submit a valid Claude share or artifact URL, plus your LinkedIn post URL when prompted.

dayContent.title: Client Proposals
dayContent.tagline: A full project proposal that wins the deal

learning.summary: How to generate a complete client proposal: scope, timeline, deliverables, and pricing. You'll learn to structure persuasion the way consultants do.

bullets.label: Proposal anatomy
bullets.text: problem, solution, scope, timeline, ROI, pricing, next steps.

bullets.label: Value framing
bullets.text: how to make pricing feel like value, not cost.

bullets.label: Slide-ready
bullets.text: how to generate a slide-style proposal you can later turn into a deck.

tool.name: Showcase
tool.description: Share the proposal you created and walk people through how AI helped you build it fast.
setupSteps: Invent or use a real service you could offer (design, tutoring, web dev, consulting).
Generate a full proposal with the prompt template.
Refine pricing and timeline until they feel realistic.
Share the finished proposal (blur private details) and how long it would have taken without AI.

task.title: Your Task
steps: Invent or use a real service you could offer (design, tutoring, web dev, consulting).
Use the template to generate a full proposal for an imagined or real client.
Refine the pricing and timeline sections until they feel realistic.

dayContent.promptTemplate:
Create a client-ready proposal.
Service: [your AI / service offering].  Client: [industry].
Generate slide titles + 3 concise bullet points each:
  1. Title   2. About Us   3. Problem   4. Solution
  5. How It Works   6. Case Study   7. Implementation
  8. ROI   9. Why Us   10. Next Steps
Keep it concise and professional.

engagement.description: Share your finished proposal (blur any private details). Tell people how long it would have taken you without AI vs how long it actually took.

deliverable.description: A full project proposal with scope, timeline & pricing

title: Day 9: Meeting Summaries & MoM
problemStatement:

**Theme:** Phase 2 — Business Communication & Email Writing

**Today's focus:** Meeting Summaries & Minutes of Meeting

**Tasks:**
1. Turn messy meeting notes into clean Minutes of Meeting.
2. Build a clear action-item table with owners and due dates.
3. Prepare your share link or notes for submission.

**Submission:** Share a Claude conversation link, artifact, or short doc that shows your work.

**Note:** Real content from our team launches before June 1, 2026.
learningObjectives: Apply Day 9 concepts to your real role or studies
Build repeatable prompts you can reuse after the challenge
Practice safe, reviewable AI workflows
linkedinTemplate:
Day 9/60 of #60DayClaudeChallenge with @ABtalks 🚀

Today (Meeting Summaries & MoM): fed Claude messy notes and got back clean minutes with owners and due dates. Chaos → order.

How much time do meetings eat in your week? 👇

#60DayClaudeChallenge #ABtalks
solutionApproach: Submit a valid Claude share or artifact URL, plus your LinkedIn post URL when prompted.

dayContent.title: Meeting Summaries & MoM
dayContent.tagline: Turn messy notes into clean minutes

learning.summary: How to convert raw meeting notes or a transcript into clean Minutes of Meeting (MoM) with decisions, action items, and owners. You'll learn to extract structure from chaos.

bullets.label: Chaos to structure
bullets.text: how to feed Claude raw, messy notes and get back structured minutes.

bullets.label: Standard format
bullets.text: attendees, agenda, decisions, action items with owners and due dates.

bullets.label: Specific actions
bullets.text: how to make every action item specific and assigned.

tool.name: Showcase
tool.description: Share the polished MoM you generated from rough notes.
setupSteps: Use notes from any real or sample meeting (a class group project works great).
Paste the raw notes into Claude with the prompt template.
Generate a clean MoM with an action-item table.
Share the messy notes next to the clean MoM — a powerful “AI saved me time” moment.

task.title: Your Task
steps: Use notes from any real or sample meeting (a class group project works great).
Paste the raw notes into Claude using the template below.
Generate a clean MoM document with a clear action-item table.

dayContent.promptTemplate:
Convert these raw meeting notes into professional Minutes of Meeting.
Notes: [paste your messy notes here].

Output sections:
  - Meeting title, date, attendees
  - Key discussion points (bulleted)
  - Decisions made
  - Action items as a table: Task | Owner | Due date
Keep it concise and skimmable.

engagement.description: Share your messy notes next to the clean MoM Claude produced. People love seeing chaos turned into order — it's a powerful “AI saved me time” moment.

deliverable.description: A Minutes-of-Meeting document with an action-item table

title: Day 10: Executive Communication
problemStatement:
Welcome to **Day 10** of the 60-Day Claude AI Mastery Challenge.

**Theme:** Phase 2 — Business Communication & Email Writing

**Today's focus:** Executive Communication

**Tasks:**
1. Write a board-ready executive summary of ~500 words.
2. Lead with the conclusion, then support it.
3. Prepare your share link or notes for submission.

**Submission:** Share a Claude conversation link, artifact, or short doc that shows your work.

**Note:** Real content from our team launches before June 1, 2026.
learningObjectives: Apply Day 10 concepts to your real role or studies
Build repeatable prompts you can reuse after the challenge
Practice safe, reviewable AI workflows
linkedinTemplate:
Day 10/60 of #60DayClaudeChallenge with @ABtalks 🚀

Today (Executive Communication): compressed a complex topic into a 500-word board-ready summary that a busy exec gets in 30 seconds.

What's the hardest thing to summarize in your field? 👇

#60DayClaudeChallenge #ABtalks
solutionApproach: Submit a valid Claude share or artifact URL, plus your LinkedIn post URL when prompted.

dayContent.title: Executive Communication

learning.summary: How to write a board-ready executive summary report of around 500 words — dense, clear, and decision-focused. You'll learn to write for senior readers who have no time.

bullets.label: Summary pyramid
bullets.text: lead with the conclusion, then support it.

bullets.label: Compression
bullets.text: how to compress a complex topic into ~500 high-signal words.

bullets.label: Clear close
bullets.text: how to end with a clear recommendation and next steps.

tool.name: Showcase
tool.description: Share your executive summary and the topic it covers.
setupSteps: Choose a topic you understand (a project, a trend, a business idea).
Generate a 500-word board-ready summary with the prompt template.
Tighten it until every sentence earns its place.
Share it and ask: would a busy executive get the point in 30 seconds?

task.title: Your Task
steps: Choose a topic you understand (a project, a trend, a business idea).
Use the template to generate a 500-word board-ready executive summary.
Tighten it until every sentence earns its place.

dayContent.promptTemplate:
Write a board-ready executive summary (about 500 words).
Topic: [your topic].  Audience: senior executives / board.
Structure:
  - Opening: the single most important takeaway first
  - Background: 2-3 sentences of essential context
  - Key findings: 3-4 tight points
  - Recommendation + next steps
Tone: confident, concise, no jargon. Hard limit ~500 words.

engagement.description: Share your executive summary and the topic. Ask: “Would a busy executive get the point in 30 seconds?” Invite feedback on clarity.
